title: ptelea trifoliata subsp. coahuilensis
intro: Ptelea trifoliata subsp. coahuilensis, also known as Coahuila prickly ash, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae and is native to northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The health benefits of Ptelea trifoliata subsp. coahuilensis include its ability to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
